findings.evidence: TauCeti line 38 calls `ncard_primesOver_mul_ramificationIdxIn_mul_inertiaDegIn P B G`; Mathlib declares it for `(p : Ideal A) [p.IsPrime]` at `Mathlib/NumberTheory/RamificationInertia/Galois.lean:216-222`.
findings.fix: Change the hypothesis to `[P.IsPrime]` and update the docstring from nonzero maximal ideals to prime ideals; derive maximal/rational-prime special cases downstream as needed.
findings.issue: `ncard_primesOver_eq_natCard_iff_of_isGaloisGroup` requires `[P.IsMaximal]`, but the proof only calls Mathlib's prime-ideal fundamental identity, so the public API is stricter than the natural upstream level.

summary: The public Dedekind-domain wrapper remains under-generalized relative to the refactored Mathlib theorem it wraps. Its maximal-ideal assumption should be weakened to the prime-ideal level now supported upstream.
